hub_subtitle: Business English para as reuniões com o time Global da Sanofi, congressos médicos e a apresentação de agosto

lesson.menu_title: Networking in English -- Starting and Keeping a Conversation
lesson.menu_desc: Como abrir, manter e fechar uma conversa em inglês num evento global: o coquetel, o jantar e o momento de trocar contatos -- 35 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 9 -- Networking in English
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Emmanuele Orrico | Lesson 9 -- Networking in English
lesson.grammar_point: present simple, present continuous and going to in conversation (review)

listenings.text: Good morning, everyone, and welcome to the Global Immunology Conference. My name is Robert, and I am part of the organizing team. Here is some information for your day. The first session starts at nine o'clock, in the main room. Our first speaker is Doctor Silva, from São Paulo, and she is going to talk about new treatments in dermatology. After lunch, we have a panel with four experts. They are going to discuss market access in Latin America. And tonight, at seven, we have a networking dinner. This is a great moment to meet new colleagues and to catch up with the old ones. So please, do not stay alone. Go and break the ice. Enjoy the conference.

Hi Emmanuele, it's Claire, from the Global team. I saw your name on the conference list, and it is so good to know you are coming. Listen, I am going to be at the dinner tonight, and I really want to catch up with you. We only see each other once in a while, so let's not miss it. Also, I want to mention one thing. The VP of Immunology is going to be there too, and he loves to talk about the Brazilian market. It is a great chance to break the ice with him. Come and find me at seven. See you tonight.

extra_audio.text: Hi, I'm Emmanuele. Nice to meet you. Have you been to this conference before? I work at Sanofi Brazil. Right now, we are launching a new product in dermatology. It was great to catch up. Let's stay in touch.

items.situation: You are at the coffee break, next to someone you do not know. Break the ice.
tips: Hi, I'm Emmanuele. Have you been to this conference before?
Hi, nice to meet you. Is this your first time here?
I'm the national demand manager at Sanofi Brazil.
I work at Sanofi Brazil. I'm responsible for immunology.

items.situation: You want to know why the person is at the event.
tips: Are you here for the immunology panel?

items.situation: A Global colleague asks about your current project.
tips: Right now, we are launching a new product in dermatology.
At the moment, we are working on the private market.

items.situation: "Are you going to present something in August?"
tips: Yes, I'm going to present the results to the Global team.
Yes. In August, I'm going to present the Brazilian market.

items.situation: The person has to leave. Close the conversation in a warm, professional way.
tips: It was great to catch up. Let's stay in touch.
It was nice to meet you. Let's stay in touch.
